message:
feat(packages): implement full Browser Edition SDK surface with React and Next.js adapters

Replace the minimal re-export stubs with complete, production-grade SDK
implementations across all four consumer packages:

- packages/browser/src/index.ts: Major expansion (~600 lines) implementing
  the high-level Browser Edition SDK. Wraps @asupersync/browser-core raw
  bindings with ergonomic TypeScript APIs including: deterministic runtime
  initialization with ABI version negotiation, region/scope lifecycle
  management, task spawning with structured cancellation, budget creation
  and enforcement, fetch capability with policy authorization, and
  comprehensive diagnostics/tracing hooks. All public APIs are fully typed
  with JSDoc documentation.

- packages/react/src/index.ts: Add React hooks and context providers —
  useAsupersyncRuntime() for runtime lifecycle management within React
  component trees, useTask() for spawning cancellation-aware async tasks,
  useRegion() for scoped resource management, and AsupersyncProvider
  context for sharing runtime instances.

- packages/next/src/index.ts: Add Next.js App Router integration —
  hydration-safe runtime initialization that defers WASM loading until
  client-side hydration completes, server/client boundary detection,
  and App Router-compatible component wrappers.

- packages/browser-core/package.json: Update exports and dependency
  declarations to match the expanded API surface consumed by the
  higher-level packages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
